title: Solemn Declaration Concerning a Citizenship Certificate that was Lost, Stolen, Destroyed or Never Received - Canada.ca
content:
## Solemn Declaration Concerning a Citizenship Certificate that was Lost, Stolen, Destroyed or Never Received
Get help to open an application form or to fill one out in the Help Centre .
This package includes the following item:
- Solemn Declaration Concerning a Citizenship Certificate that was Lost, Stolen, Destroyed or Never Received (CIT 0457) (opens in new tab)
- you applied for a Canadian citizenship certificate and it’s been more than 6 weeks since we mailed it to you, but you haven’t received it If it’s been more than 180 days , you must apply for a new certificate . To find out when it was mailed, check your file online .
- If it’s been more than 180 days , you must apply for a new certificate .
- To find out when it was mailed, check your file online .
- your Canadian citizenship certificate was lost, stolen or destroyed You must submit a declaration, even if you don’t plan to replace it. We recommend you apply for a new citizenship certificate .
- You must submit a declaration, even if you don’t plan to replace it.
- We recommend you apply for a new citizenship certificate .
Read more about the citizenship certificate .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
